Here comes Chris Cenac Jr., who’s about to square off against his former teammate with the Houston Cougars, Emmanuel Sharp.

items.title: When you speak to someone about Chris Cenac Jr., those who know him rave about his work ethic & determination.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
When you speak to someone about Chris Cenac Jr., those who know him rave about his work ethic & determination.

His burning desire to be his best self is why he left home for Link Academy in MO. It's why he thrived under Kelvin Sampson. And it's why the Celtics are investing in his pursuit.

As he readies to anchor down in Boston, Chris Cenac Jr. has a message to Celtics fans before he arrives.

Chris Cenac Jr. gets the start for the Summer League Celtics today.

It appears like yesterday was Hugo Gonzalez's last game in Las Vegas. Amari Williams is also out today.

Today's starters vs. the Hawks:

Curtis Jones 
John Tonje 
Tucker DeVries 
Dillon Mitchell 
Chris Cenac Jr

items.title: With Mitchell Robinson & Mike Conley in the fold, the Celtics are at 15 players on standard deals (including Chris Cenac). I've got Boston at $3.5M over the tax and $5.1M under their first-apron hard cap (triggered by signing Robinson with the NTMLE).
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
With Mitchell Robinson & Mike Conley in the fold, the Celtics are at 15 players on standard deals (including Chris Cenac). I've got Boston at $3.5M over the tax and $5.1M under their first-apron hard cap (triggered by signing Robinson with the NTMLE).

Enough flexibility for other moves this summer.

items.title: Chris Cenac Jr. and Jordan Walsh are both alums of Link Academy (MO).
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr. and Jordan Walsh are both alums of Link Academy (MO).

I asked Cenac if he knew Walsh before joining the Celtics.

“I didn't, but I'm looking forward to talking to him about his experience there for sure.”

items.title: Coach Sampson said that Brad Stevens was probably the one from the NBA who talked to him about Chris Cenac Jr. the most.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Coach Sampson said that Brad Stevens was probably the one from the NBA who talked to him about Chris Cenac Jr. the most.

I asked Cenac about Coach Sampson's message to him about going to the Celtics?

“I'm coming into a great organization and coming into a place where it's a great fit for me.”

items.title: Chris Cenac Jr., who, along with Dillon Mitchell, arrived in Boston yesterday, on getting introduced to those within the Celtics’ organization:
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr., who, along with Dillon Mitchell, arrived in Boston yesterday, on getting introduced to those within the Celtics’ organization:

“It feels like home already.”

items.title: Chris Cenac Jr. on his introduction to Boston:
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr. on his introduction to Boston:

“It’s been nice, very welcoming. The fans are showing a lot of love.”

items.title: I asked Chris Cenac Jr. what it is about hard coaching and the way Kelvin Sampson challenged him at Houston that brings the best out of him.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
I asked Chris Cenac Jr. what it is about hard coaching and the way Kelvin Sampson challenged him at Houston that brings the best out of him.

“It just makes you want to be better.”

items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr. was a consenus 5-star recruit & ranked No. 7 in the nation. He chose to go to a demanding, hard-nosed program in Houston.

What the 19-year-old showed under Kelvin Sampson, combined with his physical & athletic gifts is why the Celtics bet on what he'll do in their infrastructure.

items.title: The Celtics select Chris Cenac Jr. with the No. 27 pick.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
The Celtics select Chris Cenac Jr. with the No. 27 pick.

Cenac is raw, but the 19-year-old is 6-11, athletic, is a mobile big, could grow into a rim protector as well as a floor-spacer.

This is a high-upside pick for Boston.
